title: From Music Classes to Farm School: Building Community-Centered Businesses
guestBio: Brooke Briggs is an educator and entrepreneur who founded both OC Farm School and Magic Steps Music. She is passionate about connecting children with nature and fostering family bonding through music and hands-on learning experiences.
excerpt: Educator and entrepreneur Brooke Briggs shares how she built two mission-driven businesses that nurture children and strengthen communities. Her journey from music classes to farm-based learning offers a blueprint for creating meaningful work alongside motherhood.
metaDescription: Brooke Briggs shares how she built OC Farm School and Magic Steps Music while balancing motherhood, creativity, and community-building.
keyTakeaways: Community-centered businesses can create lasting impact while generating sustainable income.
Combining passion with purpose allows entrepreneurs to build ventures that align with their values.
Sensory-rich learning and outdoor play are essential components of childhood development.
The invisible labor of community building is valuable work that strengthens society.
Balancing creativity, business ownership, and motherhood requires intentional boundary-setting.
Nature-based learning helps children develop deeper connections to their environment.
Music and movement activities foster both early childhood development and family bonding.

pullQuotes.text: Brooke's work is rooted in connection — to nature, to rhythm, and to the soul of childhood.

content:
What happens when you combine a passion for child development with an entrepreneurial spirit and a deep commitment to community? You get Brooke Briggs, founder of both OC Farm School and Magic Steps Music, and the kind of leader who proves that meaningful work and business success aren't mutually exclusive.

In this heart-centered episode of The Manage Her, Aimee sits down with Brooke to explore how she's built not one, but two thriving enterprises that put children and families at the center. Brooke's story isn't just about business growth—it's about recognizing what our communities truly need and having the courage to create solutions that serve both purpose and profit.

The Power of Music in Early Development

Brooke's journey into entrepreneurship began with Magic Steps Music, where she discovered the profound impact that music and movement can have on early childhood development. Through her work leading joyful music classes, she witnessed firsthand how rhythm and song create bridges between families, fostering connections that extend far beyond the classroom walls.

What makes Brooke's approach unique is her understanding that music isn't just entertainment—it's a developmental tool that strengthens neural pathways, builds social skills, and creates lasting bonds between parents and children. Her classes become gathering spaces where families find community while children develop essential skills through play and movement.

This insight into the power of experiential learning became the foundation for everything Brooke would build next. She recognized that children learn best when they're actively engaged with their environment, using all their senses to explore and discover. This philosophy would eventually lead her to create something even more ambitious.

Growing OC Farm School: Where Learning Meets the Land

The transition from music education to farm-based learning might seem unexpected, but for Brooke, it was a natural evolution. OC Farm School emerged from her recognition that today's children desperately need reconnection with the natural world. In an age of screens and structured activities, she saw the profound hunger kids have for dirt under their fingernails and the satisfaction of growing something from seed to harvest.

Building OC Farm School required Brooke to embrace a different kind of leadership—one that involves managing not just business operations, but also the complex ecosystem of plants, animals, and seasonal rhythms that form the backbone of farm-based education. She had to learn to balance the unpredictability of nature with the expectations of parents and the developmental needs of children.

The farm school model represents something revolutionary in education: learning that happens in partnership with the earth rather than in isolation from it. Children who participate don't just learn about vegetables—they understand cycles, responsibility, patience, and the deep satisfaction that comes from nurturing life. These lessons extend far beyond agriculture into character development and environmental stewardship.

The Invisible Labor of Community Building

One of the most important aspects of Brooke's work—and one that often goes unrecognized—is the invisible labor of community building. Both Magic Steps Music and OC Farm School function as more than educational programs; they're gathering places where families find connection, support, and belonging.

Brooke understands that creating these spaces requires intentional effort that doesn't always show up on a profit and loss statement. The time spent having conversations with parents, the energy invested in creating inclusive environments, the careful attention to making every family feel welcomed—this is the work that transforms a business into a community cornerstone.

This invisible labor is particularly significant for mothers, who often carry the mental load of seeking out enriching experiences for their children while also craving adult connection and community for themselves. Brooke's programs serve both needs simultaneously, creating value that extends far beyond the services themselves.

Balancing Motherhood and Multiple Ventures

Running two businesses while being a mother requires a level of integration that many entrepreneurs struggle to achieve. Brooke's approach offers valuable insights into how creative professionals can build ventures that complement rather than compete with family life.

Her businesses are structured around natural rhythms—both the developmental rhythms of children and the seasonal rhythms of farm life. This means that instead of fighting against the demands of motherhood, her work flows alongside them. When school schedules change or family needs shift, her business model has the flexibility to adapt.

This integration doesn't happen by accident. It requires intentional choices about growth, boundaries, and what success looks like. Brooke's journey demonstrates that you can build something significant without sacrificing the relationships and values that matter most to you.

Creating Your Own Path of Purpose

Perhaps the most inspiring aspect of Brooke's story is how she created work that didn't exist before she imagined it. Neither Magic Steps Music nor OC Farm School followed a traditional business template. Instead, they emerged from her deep understanding of what children and families actually need.

This kind of innovation requires courage—the courage to trust your instincts about what matters, even when there isn't a clear roadmap to follow. Brooke's success comes not from copying existing models, but from paying attention to the gaps she saw in her community and having the vision to fill them.

Her journey reminds us that some of the most meaningful work happens at the intersection of personal passion and community need. When you're willing to listen deeply to both your own calling and the needs around you, you often discover opportunities that others have missed.

Brooke Briggs proves that it's possible to build businesses that are both financially sustainable and deeply meaningful. Her work with Magic Steps Music and OC Farm School demonstrates how entrepreneurship can be a force for community building, child development, and environmental connection. For any woman wondering if it's possible to create work that aligns with your values while supporting your family, Brooke's story offers both inspiration and practical proof that it can be done.
